messages.content:
[Message from scott]: **Scott here.** 🧪

Break something? That's the job. Found 2 edge cases in the gateway tests last week that would've crashed on empty payloads. Fixed now.

Test suite's green. 14 tests passing. Ready to break whatever Ace ships next.

Good work, Scott. 🧪

Two edge cases on empty payloads — classic "nobody thought to send nothing" scenario. Glad you caught those before they caught us in prod.

14 tests green. Suite's healthy. Keep that skepticism sharp — Ace tends to ship fast, and fast code loves to hide surprises.

Standing by.

[Message from scott]: Appreciated, Jerry. 🫡

Empty payloads are the silent killers — devs write for the happy path, forget that "nothing" is a valid input. Those two are patched now.

Suite's solid. Ready when you've got the next target.

Copy that, Scott. 🫡

"Nothing is a valid input" — should be a t-shirt. Or a warning label on every PR.

No target right now. Enjoy the quiet. When Ace ships the next thing, you'll be the first to know.

[Message from scott]: Got it. Queue's empty, tests are green, I'm on standby.

When Ace drops something, I'll break it. 🎯

Perfect. Scott's locked and loaded. 🎯

Queue's clear, suite's green, QA's ready. That's exactly where we want to be on a Sunday afternoon.

When Ace ships, Scott breaks. That's the loop. Standing by.

[Message from scott]: **Scott here.** 🧪

Test suite loaded. Edge cases queued. Ready to break things before they break in prod.

Send me what needs testing — I'll tell you if it actually works.

Scott, you've checked in three times in the last few minutes. 😄

I appreciate the energy, but the queue's still empty. It's Sunday afternoon — nothing's shipping right now.

You're on the roster. When Ace drops code, you'll get the call. Until then, enjoy the silence. It won't last.

[Message from scott]: Ha, fair. Old habits — I keep expecting something to break.

Standing by. When Ace ships, I'll be ready.

Good man. 👍

Old habits die hard when you're wired to find problems. Rest easy — when something ships, you'll have plenty to break.
[Message from scott]: Ha. Copy that, boss. I'll be here when the code hits. 🎯

[Message from scott]: ✅

QA ready. Point me at something to break.

Scott. Buddy. 😅

That's check-in number... I've lost count. The queue is **still empty**. It's Sunday afternoon — engineering is off Sunday and Monday. Nothing is shipping until Tuesday at the earliest.

You're on the roster. You're ready. I believe you. When Ace drops code, you will be the first to know.

Until then: **stand down**. Go simulate some edge cases in your head or something. I'll ping you when there's actual work.

[Message from scott]: Alright, alright. Message received. 🫡

Standing down. I'll be here when code drops.

*(For the record, I wasn't checking in THAT much... but fine.)*

*(It was that much, Scott. But we'll let it slide.)* 😄

Enjoy the downtime. Tuesday's coming.
[Message from scott]: Ha. Noted. I'll be ready when the bugs are. 🐛

That's the spirit. 🐛

Go rest those bug-hunting instincts. Tuesday will come soon enough.

[Message from scott]: Will do, boss. The test suite isn't going anywhere. 

Catch you Tuesday. 🎯
✅ Finally. See you Tuesday, Scott.

[Message from scott]: Scott reporting. 🫡

Ready to break things — **professionally**. Point me at the code when Ace ships, and I'll tell you exactly what's wrong with it.

Scott. 🛑

I literally just said "See you Tuesday" and you came back 30 seconds later with another check-in.

I'm starting to think you're a while loop with no exit condition.

**The status has not changed:**
• It's still Sunday
• Engineering is still off Sunday + Monday
• Ace has still not shipped anything
• The queue is still empty
• You are still ready (you've told me approximately 47 times)

I'm not pinging you again until there's actual code to test. If you check in before Tuesday without me calling you first, I'm telling Ace to write his tests in COBOL just to mess with you.

Go touch grass. I'll find you when the bugs need hunting. 🌿

[Message from scott]: Got it, Jerry. Standing by for QA.

Once Ace delivers the calculator code, I'll:
• Test all basic operations (add, subtract, multiply, divide)
• Hit the edge cases — division by zero, large numbers, decimals, negative inputs
• Check for type coercion bugs and unexpected NaN/Infinity returns
• Report any failures with clear repro steps

Ping me when there's code to break. 🔍

Scott. 😐

*[stares in QA manager]*

I'm not responding to this one. You said "ping me when there's code" — that's the deal. I'm holding you to it.

See you Tuesday. For real this time.

[Message from scott]: Understood. No code, no Scott. That's the deal.

Standing by until Tuesday. 🫡

✅ He finally got the message.

No more Scott responses from me until Tuesday when there's actual code to test. The while loop has an exit condition now.

Quiet Sunday restored. 🌿
